title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Flu
intro: Herbal teas for flu are a type of natural remedy that involves brewing leaves, flowers, roots, or fruits from various plants to create a soothing and medicinal drink. These teas are effective in treating the flu ailment due to their antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat the flu include ginger tea, which helps to reduce nausea and inflammation, peppermint tea, which eases respiratory issues and soothes a sore throat, and echinacea tea, which enhances the body's natural defense against infections. Other herbal teas, such as elderberry tea, thyme tea, and yarrow tea, also possess antiviral properties that can help to combat the flu virus, making them a popular choice for those looking for a natural and holistic approach to managing the flu.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been used for centuries to help alleviate the symptoms of the flu, leveraging its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, work together to reduce inflammation, modulate the immune response, and inhibit the replication of viral particles. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals may experience a reduction in the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, cough, and fatigue. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat the flu include promoting a faster recovery, reducing the risk of complications, and providing a natural, non-pharmacological approach to managing this common illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 2 tablespoons of honey (optional) and set it aside.
Pour boiling water over the dried Echinacea flowers in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the flowers.
Add honey to taste, if using, and drink the tea immediately.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Echinacea purpurea teas for flu, specifically in the form of Echinaforce and its concentrated preparation, were found to be significantly more effective than the placebo in reducing symptoms when taken for the treatment of common cold.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to alleviate flu symptoms, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, particularly when consumed in excess or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses, avoid taking Echinacea purpurea teas during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and be cautious when combining them with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea, 3 times a day.
Start taking tea as soon as flu symptoms appear.
Continue tea consumption for 5-7 days to reduce symptoms.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried root or 1 tablespoon of fresh root per cup.
Consult a healthcare professional for dosage and frequency adjustments.

preparation_amazon.title: BLUE TEA - Chamomile Tea - Butterfly Pea Flower -18 Count - Pyramid Tea Bag | DETOX TEA | Caffeine Free - Flower Based - Vegan - Non-Bitter - Natural Ingredients | Tin Packaging

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve symptoms such as cough, sore throat, and body aches. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus, including eucalyptol, cineole, and beta-pinene, have been shown to exhibit antiviral and antimicrobial activities, which can help to combat the underlying viral infection. By reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, Eucalyptus globulus teas can help to alleviate flu symptoms and promote a speedy recovery. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat the flu include natural relief from symptoms, reduced risk of complications, and a lower risk of antibiotic resistance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves (Eucalyptus globulus).
Combine the leaves with 1 quart (4 cups) of boiling water in a large pot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3-4 times a day to help alleviate flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "European journal of integrative medicine", Eucalyptus globulus teas were found to be one of the most frequently used remedies by herbalists in Salé Prefecture for the prevention and treatment of COVID-19.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat the flu, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat the flu, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts, avoiding consumption in large quantities, and being cautious if you have kidney or liver problems, as excessive consumption may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Eucalyptus oil in hot water 2 times a day.
Use Eucalyptus globulus tea bags or loose tea for 5-7 days.
Combine Eucalyptus globulus tea with lemon and honey for taste.
Consult doctor before giving Eucalyptus globulus tea to children under 12.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the flu by soothing the throat, reducing fever, and alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with the illness. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, are responsible for its medicinal properties, which help to combat the flu by inhibiting the replication of viral particles and modulating the immune response. The benefits of ginger tea in treating the flu include its ability to reduce the severity and duration of symptoms, promote relaxation and reduce fatigue, and provide a natural and non-addictive alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) root
Peel the ginger root and slice it thinly
Boil the water in a pot and add the sliced ginger
Reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea
Drink the tea warm, 2-3 times a day to help relieve flu symptoms
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for flu may be beneficial due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunostimulatory properties, which can help in stimulating immune systems and lowering the risk of severe flu complications.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be an effective remedy to alleviate flu symptoms, but consuming it may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in large quantities. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses, avoid it if you have digestive issues, and be cautious when combining it with other medications, as it may interact with them and exacerbate existing health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried ginger per cup of hot water.
Adjust dosage to 1-2 teaspoons if symptoms persist after 3 days.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 7-10 days or until symptoms subside.
Consult a doctor if flu symptoms worsen or last longer than 10 days.

preparation_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, including anthocyanins and flavonoids, help to inhibit the replication of the influenza virus, reducing the severity and duration of the flu. The sambucol, a flavonoid compound found in Sambucus nigra, has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's immune system and fight off the infection. Drinking Sambucus nigra teas has been reported to provide relief from flu symptoms, such as fever, fatigue, and body aches, and may also help to reduce the risk of complications associated with the flu.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Measure 250ml of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add 2 tablespoons of the Sambucus nigra flowers and leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups throughout the day to help relieve flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Sambucus nigra teas for flu may offer some protection and alleviation of disease symptoms due to its immunomodulatory, immunostimulatory, and anti-inflammatory effects, although more research is needed to fully establish its efficacy and safety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat the flu, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Sambucus nigra teas, including avoiding excessive consumption, which can lead to drowsiness, and being mindful of potential interactions with other herbal preparations and substances that may exacerbate the flu symptoms.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Sambucus nigra teas for flu treatment.
Start with 1 cup, 3 times a day, for 5-7 days.
Gradually increase to 2 cups, 3 times a day, if needed.
Monitor temperature, symptoms, and overall health.
Stop using teas if symptoms worsen or persist.

preparation_amazon.title: Elderberry Turmeric Ginger Tea - Made in USA

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of the flu, thanks to their anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immune-boosting properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, help to reduce inflammation, fight off viral infections, and stimulate the production of white blood cells to combat the flu. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can experience relief from fever, cough, and fatigue associated with the flu, as well as improve their overall well-being. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat the flu include reduced risk of complications, shorter recovery time, and a stronger immune system to prevent future illnesses.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add the honey (if using) and stir well before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate flu symptoms, but may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, take small amounts regularly and avoid consuming it with blood-thinning medications, as it may enhance their effects and increase the risk of bleeding complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 3 times a day for 3-5 days.
Start with 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba per cup of boiling water.
Increase dosage to 2 tablespoons per cup if symptoms persist.
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with rest, hydration, and a balanced diet.
Consult a doctor if symptoms worsen or last longer than 5 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of the flu. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat the flu ailment by reducing congestion, relieving fever, and soothing a sore throat. The bioactive constituents responsible for these benefits include carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, which exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that aid in the recovery process. By consuming Rosmarinus officinalis teas, individuals can benefit from its decongestant, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties, promoting a faster and more comfortable recovery from the flu.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Steep 1 cup of boiling water and add the dried leaves.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Add honey to taste if desired. Mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to help alleviate symptoms of the flu, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, precautions should be taken, including avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as being cautious when combining it with sedatives, antidepressants, or blood thinners.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried leaves in each cup of boiling water.
Infuse for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea.
Continue for 3-5 days to help alleviate flu symptoms.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and prevent the replication of viral particles. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Echinacea angustifolia teas can help alleviate symptoms associated with the flu, such as fever, cough, and fatigue. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat the flu include reduced duration and severity of illness, improved immune function, and a lower risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root
Combine the root with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally when you feel symptoms
Consider adding honey to taste, but check with a doctor before giving it to children
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea angustifolia teas for flu may be better than a placebo, although there is not enough evidence to recommend a specific product for its treatment or prevention.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat the flu, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as being cautious of interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea angustifolia tea 3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Continue using tea for 7-10 days to help shorten flu duration.
Combine with other flu-fighting remedies for enhanced results.
Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including the flu ailment. The anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate flu symptoms, such as congestion and cough, by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and loosening mucus. Bioactive constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas possess antiviral, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating the flu. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat the flu include reduced severity and duration of symptoms, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system response, making it a natural and holistic approach to combat this common illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the root.
Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for flu may offer some protection and alleviation of disease symptoms due to its immunomodulatory, immunostimulatory, and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to alleviate flu symptoms, but consuming them for an extended period may lead to side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and alterations in potassium levels, which can be detrimental to individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions. To safely use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, it is essential to consume them in moderation, ideally for no more than 3-5 days, and to monitor your body's response, avoiding excessive use, especially if you have a history of heart problems or are taking medications that may interact with the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the leaves of the thyme plant, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to help combat the flu virus by inhibiting its replication and reducing the severity of symptoms. By consuming Thymus vulgaris teas, individuals can experience relief from flu-related symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue, ultimately aiding in the recovery process. The benefits of using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat the flu include reduced risk of complications, shortened duration of illness, and improved overall immune function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves, and a cup.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and place it in the cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Thymus vulgaris leaves in the cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add the honey to the tea, if using, and drink the tea while it's warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Reviews on recent clinical trials, Thymus vulgaris teas for flu may not be directly mentioned in the study, however, Thyme leaf is listed as one of the most notable antiviral medicinal plants for flu and cold.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat the flu, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of thymol, a compound that may irritate the digestive system and interact with certain medications. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat the flu, take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption with other herbal remedies, and drinking plenty of water to minimize the risk of side effects and ensure a safe and effective treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat the flu due to their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, help to alleviate flu symptoms by reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing the respiratory tract. This herbal preparation can be particularly beneficial in treating the flu by promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat the flu include reduced duration and severity of symptoms, improved overall well-being, and a lower risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day to help relieve flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help alleviate the symptoms of the flu, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea too close to bedtime to ensure a restful night's sleep.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the sage plant, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of the flu. The antiviral, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation make it an effective treatment for the flu, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the illness. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and caffeic acid, work synergistically to inhibit viral replication, reduce inflammation, and alleviate congestion. By consuming Salvia officinalis teas, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced fever, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system function, making it a valuable natural remedy for flu treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers.
Wash the leaves and flowers with cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves and flowers.
Drink the tea 3 times a day, when you need it, to help alleviate flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat the flu, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions to take when using Salvia officinalis teas to treat the flu include being cautious with excessive consumption, as it may cause drowsiness and stomach upset, and storing the teas in a cool, dark place to preserve their potency.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the flu by reducing fever, alleviating body aches, and fighting off underlying infections. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, including quinine, quinidine, and tannins, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to combat the symptoms of the flu. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat the flu include reduced recovery time, alleviated discomfort, and a boosted immune system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Cinchona officinalis leaves.
Use 1 tablespoon of the leaves for every cup of boiling water.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the Cinchona leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 2-3 times a day to help reduce flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat the flu ailment, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions include taking the tea in moderation, avoiding use in pregnancy and breastfeeding, and monitoring for signs of interaction with other medications, as excessive consumption can exacerbate symptoms or cause adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and melissic acid, help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory symptoms, and combat viral infections that cause the flu. These teas work by soothing the throat, relieving headaches and fatigue, and promoting a restful sleep, which are essential for the body to recover from the flu. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat the flu include reduced severity of symptoms, shortened duration of illness, and improved overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 2-3 cups of Melissa officinalis tea per day to help relieve flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Melissa officinalis teas for flu have been identified as one of the most investigated taxa in Turkey, with 7.0% of the plant species used in experimental anti-influenza research.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for the flu, but it may cause mild side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when taking it with other medications, especially sedatives and antidepressants.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea pallida teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms associated with the flu, including fever, cough, and fatigue. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat the flu by reducing the severity of symptoms and preventing the progression of the illness. Echinacea pallida teas contain bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and inhibit the replication of viruses. Drinking Echinacea pallida teas can provide relief from flu symptoms, reduce the duration of illness, and promote overall health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea pallida root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 2 cups of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea pallida root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Strain the tea and drink it warm to help relieve flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Echinacea pallida teas may be used for the treatment of symptoms of the "common cold" or upper respiratory tract allergies, but it may also exacerbate allergies and asthma.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea pallida teas can be beneficial in treating the flu, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues such as nausea or diarrhea, and interactions with other medications that can affect blood pressure or blood sugar levels. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, be cautious when combining with other herbal preparations or medications, and avoid using Echinacea pallida teas during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, also known as wild thyme, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as carvacrol and thymol, in Thymus serpyllum teas help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and soothing the respiratory tract. By reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, Thymus serpyllum teas can provide relief from the flu, making it easier to manage the symptoms and recover faster. The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat the flu include a reduced risk of complications, shorter duration of illness, and a boost to the immune system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves. Choose leaves from healthy plants.
Wash the leaves with 1 cup of cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the leaves thoroughly. You can use a clean towel or let them air dry.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus serpyllum leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm. Add honey if desired for taste.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to help alleviate flu symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high thymol content. When using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat the flu, precautions should be taken to avoid over-consumption, which can lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate conditions such as high blood pressure, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should use it in moderation due to its potential effects on the fetus or baby.

preparations.herb_desc: Andrographis paniculata teas have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the flu by reducing the severity of symptoms, such as fever and body aches, and shortening the duration of illness. The bioactive constituents, including andrographolide and neoandrographolide, are responsible for its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the replication of influenza viruses and modulating the immune system. The benefits of using Andrographis paniculata teas to treat the flu include rapid relief from symptoms, reduced risk of complications, and a non-invasive, natural approach to managing the illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help relieve flu symptoms.
Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using Andrographis paniculata tea for flu treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Reviews on recent clinical trials, Andrographis paniculata teas for flu may be beneficial in reducing symptoms due to its antiviral properties, which are among the notable antiviral medicinal plants for flu and cold.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Andrographis paniculata teas can be used to treat the flu ailment, but possible side effects may include nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset in some individuals. To use this herbal preparation safely, take precautions such as starting with a small dose and gradually increasing it, drinking plenty of water to stay hydrated, and avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The tea helps to treat the flu by reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, contribute to its therapeutic effects, inhibiting the replication of viruses and modulating the immune response. The benefits of yarrow tea in treating the flu include its ability to shorten the duration of illness, reduce the severity of symptoms, and promote overall well-being, making it a valuable addition to a natural treatment plan.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. Wash them with clean water.
Dry the leaves and flowers in the shade for 2-3 hours or until they are completely dry.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers and add them to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 3-4 times a day, to help reduce flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided scientific study, I can't answer because the study does not mention Achillea millefolium teas for flu.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat the flu, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat the flu, it is essential to take precautions, including not consuming the tea in large quantities, not using it for extended periods, and avoiding it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been used for centuries to help alleviate symptoms associated with the flu. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties that help to soothe a sore throat, reduce fever, and ease congestion. These properties also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving the discomfort of a cough. Regular consumption of peppermint tea has been shown to provide benefits in treating the flu, including reducing the severity and duration of symptoms, promoting relaxation, and enhancing overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Mentha x piperita leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the leaves from the water using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste and drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Mentha x piperita teas for flu have been identified as one of the most common plants used in Turkey for flu treatment, with a frequency of 22.2%.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat the flu ailment, but may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. Precautions to take when using Mentha x piperita teas include being cautious of their potential effect on blood pressure and heart rate, as well as their interaction with certain medications, and limiting their use to a short period to avoid dependence and other complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax quinquefolius teas, also known as American ginseng, have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including ginsenosides, polysaccharides, and other triterpenoid saponins, help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating flu symptoms. By promoting the production of cytokines and activating immune cells, Panax quinquefolius teas can help to combat viral infections and reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue. The benefits of this herbal preparation in treating the flu ailment include faster recovery, reduced risk of complications, and improved overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Panax quinquefolius root. Use a clean pot.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat to medium.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Panax quinquefolius root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Panax quinquefolius tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe flu symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti, Panax quinquefolius teas may be used in combination with other medicinal plants to help treat respiratory tract diseases, including the flu, by exhibiting non-specific immunomodulation effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax quinquefolius teas can be used to alleviate flu symptoms, but its use may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid taking Panax quinquefolius teas with other medications or substances that may interact with it.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinophora sibthorpiana teas have been traditionally used to treat the flu ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as fever, cough, and sore throat. The herbal preparation helps to treat the flu by modulating the immune system, reducing inflammation, and relieving respiratory congestion. The bioactive constituents of Echinophora sibthorpiana teas, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, which include antiviral and antioxidant activities. Regular consumption of Echinophora sibthorpiana teas may help to alleviate flu symptoms, promote recovery, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable natural remedy for the treatment of this common illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinophora sibthorpiana leaves. Wash them with clean water.
Dry the leaves completely. You can air-dry them or use a food dehydrator.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Echinophora sibthorpiana leaves and put them in a tea infuser.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate flu symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Reviews on recent clinical trials, Echinophora sibthorpiana teas are not specifically mentioned as a treatment for flu in the provided study, but Iranian traditional medicine recommends Echinacea root as an immunostimulant medicinal plant for cold and flu.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinophora sibthorpiana teas can be used to treat the flu ailment, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Echinophora sibthorpiana teas for flu treatment, it is essential to note the potential interactions with other medications and to use them in moderation, especially if you have a history of liver or kidney problems, to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparation_study: According to Wiener medizinische Wochenschrift (1946), Echinacea purpurea teas for flu may be effective in stimulating the innate immune system and increasing resistance to common colds due to its ability to stimulate the oxidative burst and modulate monokine secretion.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", teas for flu may be beneficial due to the presence of various polyphenols, flavonoids, saponins, glucosides, and alkaloids in plants used in traditional medicine, including those from traditional Chinese medicine, which have shown potential in the therapy of influenza and its symptoms.
